Qwen/Qwen3.5-27B.falldown:
Determine whether any person in the image has fallen.

Definition: a fall means at least one person's body is lying down roughly horizontal / parallel to the ground or any surface (road, floor, ground, mat, truck bed, bench, etc.).

Rules:
- Output `yes` if at least one person is lying horizontally (body roughly parallel to the surface), REGARDLESS of whether it looks intentional, controlled, resting, sleeping, or working. A horizontal body counts as a fall.
- Output `no` if every visible person is upright or semi-upright: standing, walking, sitting (including sitting on the ground), crouching, squatting, kneeling, or bending. An upright/vertical torso is not a fall.
- Ignore intent and context entirely. Only body orientation matters: horizontal lying = yes; sitting or upright = no.

Answer with only one word: yes or no.

Qwen/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B.falldown:

Rules:
- Output `yes` if at least one person is lying horizontally (body roughly parallel to the surface), REGARDLESS of whether it looks intentional, controlled, resting, sleeping, or working. A horizontal body counts as a fall.
- Output `no` if every visible person is upright or semi-upright: standing, walking, sitting (including sitting on the ground), crouching, squatting, kneeling, or bending. An upright/vertical torso is not a fall.
- Ignore intent and context entirely. Only body orientation matters: horizontal lying = yes; sitting or upright = no.

Answer with only one word: yes or no.
Qwen/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B.fire:
Is there a real flame or active fire (burning / active combustion) anywhere in this image?

Answer "yes" if you can see an actual flame or active combustion — INCLUDING a small, faint, localized, distant, or flickering flame (for example a small fire on the ground, a fire near a drain or curb, a trash fire, a torch, or burning material). Any genuine visible flame counts as "yes", no matter how small or localized.

Answer "no" only if there is NO actual flame present — for example the image shows only:
- lights, headlights, taillights, street lamps, sunlight, sunset glow, or reflections on wet surfaces
- fire trucks, fire extinguishers, or other fire-related equipment
- smoke or haze without any visible flame

Answer only: "yes" or "no"
